jokes.text: Larry and actress stumble through lines: 'Oh... you... have... huh? Oh, you have, have you?' - complete breakdown of basic dialogue
jokes.setup: Actors rehearsing what should be simple opening lines
jokes.punchline: Both completely fall apart trying to deliver basic dialogue
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David moment - turns mundane theater rehearsal into cringe comedy through his inability to handle basic social/professional situations

jokes.text: 'This is how-- it's very obvious why you don't want me to go.' - Cheryl immediately suspicious about Cady Huffman
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Cady Huffman will be on the plane
jokes.punchline: Cheryl instantly sees through any innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Cheryl's realistic skepticism about Larry's behavior around other women

jokes.text: 'What? Come on, she hates my guts. I met her at Ben Stiller's party. She couldn't stand me.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl thinks Larry wants to avoid bringing her because of attraction to Cady
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals Cady actually hates him, making Cheryl's suspicion absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David irony - the truth is the opposite of what seems obvious, and Larry's social awkwardness is the real issue

jokes.text: 'You better work fast, my friend. If you're gonna cash in on this, uh, 10th anniversary gift-- you have, what, three days left?'
jokes.setup: Larry has some kind of anniversary arrangement with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Jeff treats it like a business deadline with sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Jeff's crude pragmatism about Larry's marriage creates uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: 'She has obsessive-compulsive disorder.' 'Really?' 'I-I-I told her that you have it, too.'
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals Cady has OCD
jokes.punchline: Jeff has already told this lie about Larry having it too
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff - Jeff's casual revelation that he's already committed Larry to an elaborate lie

jokes.text: 'What the-- are you nuts? What's the matter with you?' 'Come on, that's funny.'
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Jeff has lied about him having OCD
jokes.punchline: Jeff genuinely thinks this deception is hilarious
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Larry's horror vs Jeff's amusement at social manipulation

jokes.text: '123 steps that way.' '123 steps I'll be taking.'
jokes.setup: Cady asks for directions to coffee
jokes.punchline: Both respond with obsessive precision about exact step count
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - both characters instantly inhabiting fake OCD personas through precise step counting

jokes.text: 'I'd give you five, but I don't want to touch your hand. I'd have to wash it.' 'Yeah, me too.'
jokes.setup: Larry and Cady bonding over fake shared OCD
jokes.punchline: Both commit to elaborate avoidance behaviors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake OCD premise with specific behavioral details

jokes.text: 'That whole one bag is devoted to soap.'
jokes.setup: Discussing OCD travel challenges
jokes.punchline: Cady has an entire bag just for soap
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd detail that sells the fake OCD premise

jokes.text: 'Do you have anything to read?' 'I just sit here.' 'You don't have a magazine or a book, nothing?' 'No, I just sit.'
jokes.setup: Schwimmer asking about reading material on plane
jokes.punchline: Larry's monk-like ability to just sit with no stimulation
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Larry's unusual relationship with boredom/stimulation

jokes.text: Flight attendant confrontation over seat position - Larry becomes convinced Schwimmer reported him
jokes.setup: Flight attendant asks Larry to put seat upright
jokes.punchline: Larry turns routine request into paranoid investigation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry paranoia escalating routine interaction into investigation. Very true to character.

jokes.text: Schwimmer claiming his conversation with the flight attendant is 'privileged'
jokes.setup: Larry wants to know what they discussed
jokes.punchline: Schwimmer treats airplane small talk like attorney-client privilege
jokes.explanation: Absurd legalistic language applied to trivial situation. Good quotable moment.

jokes.text: 'That's my business.' 'Just give me a snippet.' 'No, the discussion I had is privileged.'
jokes.setup: Larry demands to know what Schwimmer said to flight attendant
jokes.punchline: Schwimmer treats casual airline conversation like attorney-client privilege
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation - treating airline small talk like classified information

jokes.text: Hotel tipping anxiety with bellman Tim - Larry promises to tip later, detects skepticism
jokes.setup: Larry has no small bills to tip bellman
jokes.punchline: Larry becomes paranoid about Tim's perceived doubt in his promise
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social anxiety about perceived judgment, sets up tipping subplot

jokes.text: 'Split it with Tim. You know Tim outside?' 'Split it with Tim?' 'Yeah, give Tim 10.'
jokes.setup: Larry tips hotel clerk $20
jokes.punchline: Larry creates elaborate tip-sharing arrangement between hotel employees
jokes.explanation: Larry's attempt to solve tipping anxiety creates more complex social obligation

jokes.text: Hotel room explanation - 'There's a television, here. Just open these. And you have remote control for power on and off... channel up and down'
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk giving room tour
jokes.punchline: Explains television and remote control as if Larry has never seen them
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd over-explanation of basic hotel amenities

jokes.text: 'And that's a window?' 'Yes.' 'You walk over to it and you can look out.' 'Is that what it's for?' 'Basically, yes.'
jokes.setup: Clerk continuing over-detailed room explanation
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically asks for explanation of what a window does
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd over-explanation theme with Larry's sarcastic participation

jokes.text: 'The floor is for standing.'
jokes.setup: Larry continuing the over-explanation mockery
jokes.punchline: Larry provides his own absurdly obvious room feature explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation - Larry joins the absurd over-explanation game

jokes.text: More tipping anxiety with bellman - 'I'm supposed to give you a 20? The guy will wind up with more money than me if everybody gave him a 20.'
jokes.setup: Bellman expects tip for bringing luggage
jokes.punchline: Larry calculates bellman would out-earn guests with $20 tips
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical but socially awkward analysis of tipping economics

jokes.text: Jeff pressuring Larry about the anniversary gift deadline while Larry wants to study lines
jokes.setup: Larry has legitimate Broadway concerns
jokes.punchline: Jeff prioritizes Larry's sex life over his career
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Jeff's crude priorities and Larry's torn loyalties

jokes.text: 'What kind of business are you in?' 'Grooming. Pet grooming.' 'We have a truck and we shampoo dogs.'
jokes.setup: Woman at bar asks what Larry and Jeff do
jokes.punchline: They claim to run mobile pet grooming business
jokes.explanation: Standard absurd lie setup, functional but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: 'I'm a fellatio teacher.' 'Good, very good. Okay, now, let's be honest here.'
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Christina does for work
jokes.punchline: She claims to be a fellatio instructor
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that catches Larry off guard, good cringe setup for what follows

jokes.text: Larry's tic excuse: 'I have a tic. I'm going on stage with a tic.'
jokes.setup: Christina asks about Larry's facial expression
jokes.punchline: Larry blames a nonexistent tic for his obvious lying face
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible at lying and makes it worse by inventing medical excuses

jokes.text: 'I don't know how anybody could do that without a blindfold. That's crazy. It's a grotesque monster, that penis.'
jokes.setup: Christina explains her alleged profession
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified reaction to oral sex concept
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David horror at sexual topics - perfectly in character and shockingly funny

jokes.text: 'Did you know that spicy food makes a man's come taste delicious?'
jokes.setup: Discussion about spicy food
jokes.punchline: Christina connects it to sexual graphic detail
jokes.explanation: Shock value escalation that sets up Larry's physical reaction

jokes.text: Larry chokes on water during explicit conversation - visual gag of his discomfort
jokes.setup: Christina asks about his favorite part of oral sex
jokes.punchline: Larry literally chokes and spits water
jokes.explanation: Physical manifestation of Larry's sexual discomfort - good visual comedy

jokes.text: 'I'm feeling a little sick, actually.' Larry flees the dinner
jokes.setup: Increasingly explicit dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry literally becomes nauseous and leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David exit strategy - physical revulsion at sexual frankness

jokes.text: '$53? It's breakfast. Eggs, pancakes, juice, coffee-- $53?'
jokes.setup: Larry receives room service bill
jokes.punchline: Outrageous price for basic breakfast items
jokes.explanation: Relatable hotel price shock - good observational comedy

jokes.text: 'I call it play practice. When I was doing Pippin, we stayed after school for play practice.'
jokes.setup: Larry corrects Andy calling rehearsal 'play practice'
jokes.punchline: Andy doubles down with childhood theater reference
jokes.explanation: Andy's oblivious persistence with amateur theater terminology

jokes.text: Open houses hobby - 'You just go, you visit, you know? You just go in, you tour around pretend you're buying, but you don't.'
jokes.setup: Andy suggests visiting open houses
jokes.punchline: Andy's hobby is pretending to buy houses with no intention to purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy behavior - weird hobby that wastes everyone's time

jokes.text: 'Did you find that watch on your cart?' 'Yes.' 'I know whose watch that is.'
jokes.setup: Housekeeper cleaning Larry's room
jokes.punchline: Larry discovers she found Schwimmer's missing watch
jokes.explanation: Good plot development that sets up Larry's opportunity to be helpful

jokes.text: Doorman tip confusion - Larry discovers other doorman kept the shared tip money
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate tip-sharing arrangement from earlier
jokes.punchline: The other doorman never shared the money with Tim
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup - Larry's elaborate scheme backfired

jokes.text: 'I'm gonna get some tickets today, I'll give you a ticket.' 'How about that?' 'So we're square?' 'That's good karma.'
jokes.setup: Sanjay wants tip for AC repair
jokes.punchline: Larry offers theater ticket as tip alternative
jokes.explanation: Larry's creative solution to avoid cash tips - sets up later complications

jokes.text: Larry offering Producers tickets instead of cash tip to Sanjay
jokes.setup: Sanjay wants tip for fixing air conditioning
jokes.punchline: Larry offers Broadway tickets as bartering system
jokes.explanation: Creative problem solving that reveals Larry's performer ego and cheapness

jokes.text: Tourist photo disaster - Larry contradicts tourist's vertical photo preference, couple starts fighting
jokes.setup: Larry offers to take couple's photo
jokes.punchline: His artistic input destroys their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - his helpful interference destroys strangers' relationship

jokes.text: 'Because you will fail!'
jokes.setup: Tourist discovers Larry is in Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Tourist wishes theatrical failure on Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - stranger's rage culminating in theatrical curse

jokes.text: 'What are you, nuts? You didn't even have a watch to begin with, so you're in the same position. There's no change.'
jokes.setup: Schwimmer demands Larry buy him a replacement watch
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic about net position being unchanged
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David logic - mathematically correct but morally absurd reasoning about responsibility

jokes.text: The lost watch crisis: Larry realizes he's lost Schwimmer's watch after finding it
jokes.setup: Larry triumphantly announces he found the watch
jokes.punchline: He immediately discovers he's lost it again
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - making a good deed even worse. Classic Larry self-sabotage.

jokes.text: Larry washing hands obsessively during rehearsal, cast notices his 'compulsive' behavior
jokes.setup: Larry continuing fake OCD performance from airplane
jokes.punchline: Cast interprets his nervous behavior as genuine OCD
jokes.explanation: Good callback to airplane OCD lie - Larry trapped by his own deception

jokes.text: 'I think I'm gonna bang my head against the wall, maybe 50 times.' 'You don't want to do that.' 'No, I do.'
jokes.setup: Cady describing pre-show OCD rituals
jokes.punchline: Cady insists on literally harmful ritual behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about actual mental health struggles vs Larry's fake version

jokes.text: 'You've got a picture of Bush in your dressing room?' 'Yeah.' 'You're a Republican?' 'Yes, Larry, I'm a Republican.' 'Ugh.'
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Bush photo in Cady's dressing room
jokes.punchline: Larry's visible revulsion at her political affiliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - his liberal politics creating instant revulsion

jokes.text: 'What the fuck were you thinking?!' 'A picture of Bush, who gives a flying fuck? I'd fuck her with a Bush mask on!'
jokes.setup: Larry horrified by Cady's Republican politics
jokes.punchline: Jeff's crude dismissal of political differences for sex
jokes.explanation: Jeff's characteristic crude pragmatism vs Larry's principled horror

jokes.text: 'I just need one ticket.' 'But there's a line here.' 'I'm in the show, I'm Bialystock.' 'I don't care who you are, go to the back of the line!'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to cut line at will call
jokes.punchline: Patron doesn't care that Larry is the star
jokes.explanation: Larry's assumption that being in the show grants privilege backfires

jokes.text: 'One of the maintenance guys from the hotel had it on. Out in the lobby. I had to wrestle him for it.'
jokes.setup: Larry returns Schwimmer's watch
jokes.punchline: Larry physically fought hotel employee to retrieve it
jokes.explanation: Good payoff - Larry going to absurd lengths to make amends

jokes.text: 'Just for my own edification... what did you say to that stewardess? Did you tell her about the seat not being upright?'
jokes.setup: Final moment before show starts
jokes.punchline: Larry still obsessing over airplane incident
jokes.explanation: Perfect final callback - Larry's inability to let go of perceived slights even in crucial moments

jokes.text: Larry's stage debut line delivery: 'Scared, can't talk.'
jokes.setup: Larry's entrance in the actual play
jokes.punchline: His line perfectly describes his real emotional state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy where the script line matches reality. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Larry forgets his lines on stage: 'What was I saying?' 'You were saying that under the right circumstances--' repeated multiple times
jokes.setup: Larry performing in The Producers on opening night
jokes.punchline: Complete brain freeze leading to repetitive loop
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Larry's theatrical disaster in front of Broadway audience

jokes.text: Larry's crowd work during his meltdown: addressing his cousin Andy and the Sikh's turban
jokes.setup: Larry completely breaks character during the play
jokes.punchline: He does stand-up comedy about the audience members
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where Larry abandons the play for actual stand-up. The anti-Semitism line is shocking and perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: Larry improvises insults about cousin Andy: 'the product of incest... The Primary Reason for Anti-Semitism... subletting his mouth'
jokes.setup: Larry completely off-script, sees Andy causing disruption
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal attacks disguised as crowd work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvised cruelty - Larry turning theatrical disaster into vicious personal attack on family member

jokes.text: Audience reaction twist: 'So far this is about the funniest thing I've seen on Broadway.' 'I've never laughed so hard in my life.'
jokes.setup: Larry's complete stage disaster
jokes.punchline: Audience thinks it's intentionally brilliant comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Larry's genuine failure interpreted as genius, classic Larry David paradox
